decision_algorithm: hybrid_rerank is default-enable-eligible iff, evaluated only on the non-holdout queries against the frozen corpus: ndcg@10 >= min_quality.ndcg@10.hybrid_rerank AND mrr >= min_quality.mrr.hybrid_rerank AND recall@50 >= min_quality.recall@50.hybrid_rerank at every depth in depths, AND p95 latency <= max_latency_ms.p95.hybrid_rerank, AND error_rate <= max_error_rate, AND zero_result_rate <= max_zero_result_rate, AND the commercial-egress fraction <= max_commercial_egress_fraction (i.e. no source text left the host on the lexical_only path and any commercial provider egress stays within the frozen bound). Each arm must also clear its own per-arm min_quality entries to be reported as passing; failing any single predicate fails the gate for that arm. holdout_unused_required=true means the run is invalid if any holdout query id was used for tuning or threshold selection. These numeric thresholds are the FROZEN gate: a reviewer MAY tune the values before provider work begins, but the file and its sha256 recorded in MANIFEST.json are the binding contract.
